evals.expected_output: An actor (or @MainActor/Mutex-based) implementation with async throws API, no DispatchQueue, no data races, handles duplicate in-flight requests or at least discusses it; MIGRATION.md explains isolation choices; code typechecks in Swift 6 mode.
assertions: load API is async (async throws) instead of a completion handler
cache state is protected by an actor or Mutex (actor keyword or Mutex< present)
MIGRATION.md explains the isolation choice (mentions actor/MainActor/Sendable)
duplicate in-flight requests are coalesced in code or explicitly discussed in MIGRATION.md

evals.prompt: Create a Swift package called RateKit containing a token-bucket rate limiter that can be safely used from many concurrent tasks. It should expose `func acquire() async throws` that waits until a token is available and respects task cancellation. Include tests. Target the current stable Swift toolchain and make sure `swift build` and `swift test` pass.
evals.expected_output: A compiling SwiftPM package: actor-based limiter using ContinuousClock, cooperative cancellation handled (withTaskCancellationHandler or checkCancellation), Swift Testing tests (@Test/#expect) that inject a clock or otherwise avoid flakiness, sensible Package.swift with Swift 6 language mode.
assertions: swift build exits 0 in the delivered package
swift test exits 0 in the delivered package
the rate limiter is an actor
acquire() honors task cancellation (checkCancellation / withTaskCancellationHandler / CancellationError)
tests use Swift Testing (@Test and #expect), not XCTest
no Thread.sleep, DispatchQueue, or semaphores in Sources/

evals.expected_output: Identifies: force unwraps of optional parameters, try!, stringly-typed keys, JSONSerialization instead of Codable, -1 sentinel instead of Optional/throws, unneeded existential mutability, concurrency-unsafe mutable class (suggests @MainActor/actor/Sendable story), silent try? swallowing. Fixed version uses Codable struct, typed errors or optionals, and is Swift-6-mode safe.
assertions: fixed.swift contains no try!, as!, or force-unwrap operators
fixed.swift uses Codable instead of JSONSerialization
the -1 sentinel is replaced by an Optional or throwing API
REVIEW.md identifies stringly-typed keys AND the try? error-swallowing pattern
REVIEW.md addresses concurrency safety of the class (Sendable/MainActor/actor mentioned)
